commits.body:
The homepage works, the env var sings,
Just reload your terminal for new things.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
"web" stood alone, no URL in hand,
And crashed with a usage demand.
Now it checks the env,
Falls back like a friend—
Your homepage, exactly as planned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two issues born in a single breath:
Scripts scattered far shall meet in death,
And "web" alone, no URL in tow,
Shall finally have somewhere to go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 672: Inset content by border width
commits.body:
The border was greedy, it ate
The text at the edge of each pane.
We shrank what's inside,
Gave the content a ride—
Now every last pixel is sane.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The border looked great, a fine frame,
But it covered the text—what a shame!
We'll shrink what's inside,
Push content to hide
Behind nothing. The border's to blame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The pipeline hums, the ghost looks great,
Two experiments sealed its fate.
Clean script, fresh PNGs,
Icons at all sizes please—
This issue's done. Celebrate!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 671: Regen icons from new source
commits.body:
The ghost got a makeover today,
New pixels arranged a new way.
From black-2 to black-3,
A fresh face, can't you see?
Ten PNGs, reborn from the fray.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 671: Add clean-zig script, regen icons
commits.body:
The cache was a dragon, three heads to slay,
Three rm commands to remember each day.
One script now suffices,
No Chromium sacrifices—
And fresh icons to light up the way.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 671: Plan icon update + clean script
commits.body:
A new face awaits in the wings,
A script to clean all the old things.
No more three rm commands—
Just one script in your hands—
And the icon refresh that it brings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A click meant "look here, please,"
Not "follow that link overseas."
One flag, set on focus,
Keeps the click hocus-pocus—
Now the first tap just grants you the keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
I clicked to focus, nothing more,
But followed a link to some web store.
One flag, one check—
No more train wreck—
First click just opens the door.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The colors drained from the unfocused pane,
A grayscale ghost of what it had been.
"Which pane is active?" you'll never ask again—
The saturated one. Obviously.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The colors once blazed, bright and keen,
In every unfocused pane's scene.
Now saturation shall fade,
When focus has strayed—
A grayscale ghost where color has been.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The panes once stood naked and bare,
No borders to show who was there.
A stroke in the ZStack,
Brought the focus right back—
Now each pane has a colorful flair.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 669: Revise plan — Swift overlay approach
commits.body:
We pondered Zig, we pondered Swift,
And weighed the architectural rift.
The renderer knows not "split" from "tab"—
So platform layers get the fab.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The baseline was broken all along—
Our experiments sang the wrong song.
Now resize is fixed, we try once more:
One overlay at a time, like before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The dragon slain in 666 was proud,
But dropped Resize events from the crowd.
Now Key and Resize both pass through,
While Mouse stays quiet — as mice should do.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Don't cherry-pick which events to send—
Forward them all, from start to end.
Resize, mouse, focus, paste, and more:
One channel fits what came before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The dragon slain in 666
Left resize events in the ditch.
Event::Key was all we'd keep—
The rest fell silent, lost in sleep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 667: Close — resize was already broken
commits.body:
Three experiments blamed, three experiments wrong.
The bug was here before us all along.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
updateOSView must stay empty—
That's the rule we learned the hard way.
Even borderWidth, so simple, so tempting,
Broke resize on its very first day.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
SwiftUI broke the panes? Fine, we'll go beneath—
CALayer draws the border, SwiftUI can breathe.
No modifiers, no wrappers, no effect-layer grief,
Just borderWidth and borderColor: simple relief.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We tried to dress the panes in borders bright,
With glow and saturation, quite a sight.
But SwiftUI wrapped our NSView in layers deep—
And resize, betrayed, refused to keep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five paths were weighed, from glow to gray,
Two champions emerged to stay:
A border bright, a saturation knob—
Eight config keys to do the job.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The dimmer dims, the reader squints—
We need a better set of hints.
Five paths to try, from glow to gray,
To show which pane is in play.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The devil lived in event::poll,
A quarter-second tax on soul.
Now key and XPC share one lane—
recv() blocks, and wakes with no pain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two hundred fifty milliseconds spent,
A tutorial default no one meant.
The devil's in the polling delay—
One channel shall unite the fray.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The CDN was a distant friend,
On whom we chose not to depend.
Now the font lives here with us—
No external fetch, no fuss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
No canvas net, no stretched-out grid—
A fragment shader does what they never did.
Fractal Brownian motion swirls in space,
Purple clouds and blue wisps embrace.
Per-pixel noise, no seams in sight,
THE INTERNET floats in cosmic light.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Too dim to read, too dark to see,
THE INTERNET cried, "Please notice me!"
We lit its glow from deep within—
Emissive cyan for the win.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Inline styles piled high like debt,
Each hover handler full of regret.
Now className reigns, the code is lean—
The cleanest Rick Roll you've ever seen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A label shed its camelCase,
And wore a dotcom in its place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Rick Roll modal to welcome page
commits.body:
They clicked the button, full of trust,
"Begin Your Journey" — click they must.
A modal rose, a song began,
"Never gonna give you up," sang the man.
Everybody gets Rick Rolled. Welcome.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A button glows with cyan light,
So earnest, warm, and true.
"Begin Your Journey," it invites—
We'd never let you down. Or would we?

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Brighten welcome page text and lights
commits.body:
The Internet sat dim and shy,
No one could read it passing by.
We cranked the ambient way up high—
Now even space squints at the sky.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Below the spinning chrome it reads,
In modest gray, for those with needs:
"advanced communications network
for planet earth" — nothing fancy,
just the whole world's infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Welcome to THE INTERNET page
commits.body:
Three.js spins the words in space,
Cyan light on a metallic face.
Stars drift by in cosmic night—
Welcome, friend. Enjoy the sight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Update app icon to new logo
commits.body:
The old icon served us well, it's true,
But pixels fade and tastes renew.
A fresher face now takes the stage—
The second black, the latest page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Prettier had opinions,
On where the lines should break.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Ctrl held Escape in a jealous embrace,
"You'll never leave without me!" it cried.
But Esc learned vim's wisdom, checked its own space—
Now Normal means go, Insert means abide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Five changes, three files, one key set free—
Zig drops the Ctrl, TUI checks the mode,
And hints now say what they mean to be.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The escape key sat trapped in chains,
Ctrl held it back with iron reins.
"I work alone!" Esc cried with pride—
Now Zig shall let the key decide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The issue is closed, the docs are tight,
CLAUDE.md updated, all feels right.
662 through 664 now find their place—
Each deferred or done with grace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 664 Exp 1: Replace args with clap
commits.body:
The while loop served us well, 'tis true,
But clap derives what we once hand-drew.
`web url` speaks clearly now,
`web <url>` still works — and how!
Subcommands sprout where bare strings grew.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A hand-rolled loop, so brave, so small,
Shall yield to derive macros' call.
Subcommands bloom where strings once grew—
`web url` the verb, the URL the clue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A while loop parsing args by hand—
How quaint, how fragile, how unplanned.
Now clap derives what we once wrote,
Subcommands rise; the loop's a footnote.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Close Issue 663: Defer JS context menu
commits.body:
A menu in JavaScript, simple and neat,
No focus loss, no NSMenu defeat.
But Chromium changes we'd rather postpone—
Till Zig rewrites what C++ has grown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Issue 663 Exp 1: Design JS context menu
commits.body:
A div walks into ShowContextMenu,
Styled dark, positioned true.
No NSMenu, no focus to steal—
Just DOM elements keeping it real.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
NSMenu stole focus, Zig was too late,
AppKit's event chain sealed the gate.
But a <div> in the DOM?
Stays calm, stays aplomb—
No process switch, no window debate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix 662 Exp 2: Intercept in Zig, not Swift
commits.body:
menu(for:) was never called at all—
rightMouseDown consumed the ball.
Like neovim suppressing the chain,
The overlay eats it, super's in vain.
Now Zig intercepts before the fall.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
